name: Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

tests.testPaperName: The p Block Elements CBSE Online Test 01
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: The valence shell electronic configuration of p-block elements is ns^2np^1-6. But this is not true in the case of______.
questions.question_raw: The valence shell electronic configuration of p-block elements is ns2np1-6. But this is not true in the case of______.
questions.explanation: He has 1s^2 configuration. It is a noble gas.

questions.question: There are six groups of p-block elements in the periodic table numbering from:
questions.question_raw: There are six groups of p-block elements in the periodic table numbering from:
questions.explanation: After two s block and 10 d block, p block elements starts from group 13 to group 18.

questions.question: The variation in properties in a group of p-block of elements is due to:
questions.question_raw: The variation in properties in a group of p-block of elements is due to:

options.label: C
options.text: difference in inner core of the electronic configuration.

correct_option_texts: difference in inner core of the electronic configuration.
questions.explanation: The difference in properties of p block element is because of the presence of inner lying d and f electrons which have poor shielding effect.

questions.question: The maximum oxidation state shown by any p-block element is:
questions.question_raw: The maximum oxidation state shown by any p-block element is:

options.label: A
options.text: The sum of the s electrons

options.label: C
options.text: The sum of the s and p-electrons

options.label: D
options.text: Half of the sum of the s and p-electrons

correct_option_texts: The sum of the s and p-electrons
questions.explanation: The maximum oxidation state of any p block element is the number of ionizable electrons which is equal to the sum of s and p electrons present in the valence shell. For example, the valance shell electronic configuration of group 15 element is ns^2np^3. So the maximum oxidation state is +5.

questions.question: The sum of the s and p-electrons is called:
questions.question_raw: The sum of the s and p-electrons is called:

options.label: A
options.text: The most unstable valence number of p-block elements

options.label: B
options.text: The group oxidation state of p-block elements

options.label: D
options.text: The most stable valence number of p-block elements

correct_option_texts: The group oxidation state of p-block elements
questions.explanation:
The group oxidation state is the highest oxidation state exhibited by the elements present in any particular group.
For p block elements it is the sum of the s and p-electrons.

questions.question: The inert pair effect of p-block elements is due to the:
questions.question_raw: The inert pair effect of p-block elements is due to the:

options.label: A
options.text: the most stable valence number of p-block elements.

options.label: B
options.text: the group oxidation state of p-block elements.

options.label: C
options.text: the most unstable valence number of p-block elements.

options.label: D
options.text: the inertness of the inner ns electrons because of ineffective shielding.

correct_option_texts: the inertness of the inner ns electrons because of ineffective shielding.
questions.explanation:
The inert pair effect is nothing but the extra stability concept. Generally, P-block elements show variable valiancy which is equal to the maximum oxidation (Group oxidation state) state of that group of p-block elements, p block elements may show another oxidation state i.e.+2.
Group oxidation state +4 is obtained when electrons are lost from both ns and np levels, +2 oxidation state is obtained while only np level electrons are lost due to extra stability and poor shielding of ns electron which are called inert pair. The effect is the inert effect.

questions.question: The element which exists in liquid state for a wide range of temperature and can be used for measuring high temperature is______.
questions.question_raw: The element which exists in liquid state for a wide range of temperature and can be used for measuring high temperature is______.
questions.explanation: The melting point of gallium is 30^o C and the boiling point is 2240^o C. Thus, the element exists in the liquid state for a wide range of temperatures.

questions.question: As we move from B to Al in the p-block elements the sum of the first three ionisation enthalpies:
questions.question_raw: As we move from B to Al in the p-block elements the sum of the first three ionisation enthalpies:
questions.explanation:
On moving from B to Al size of the atom increases. So ionization enthalpy decreases. Thus as we move from B to Al in the p-block elements the sum of the first three ionization enthalpies considerably decreases.
This is the reason aluminium forms Al^3+ ion.

questions.question: Boron forms only covalent compounds and not +3 ions because:
questions.question_raw: Boron forms only covalent compounds and not +3 ions because:

options.label: A
options.text: the sum of its first two ionization enthalpies is very low.

options.label: B
options.text: the sum of its first two ionization enthalpies is very high.

options.label: C
options.text: the sum of its first three ionization enthalpies is very low.

options.label: D
options.text: the sum of its first three ionization enthalpies is very high.

correct_option_texts: the sum of its first three ionization enthalpies is very high.
questions.explanation: Due to smaller size and high nuclear charge B has a very high ionization energy value so does not show a +3 oxidation state. Further according to Fajan's rule, cation with a small size and high positive charge leads to a covalent bond.

questions.question: One of the following p-block elements has unusually low melting point_________.
questions.question_raw: One of the following p-block elements has unusually low melting point_________.
questions.explanation: Ga does not do not follow any of the simple crystal structures. And two nearest Ga atoms are bonded by a covalent bond which leads to the low melting point.

questions.question: Which of the following p-block elements in group 13 could exist in a liquid state during summer?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following p-block elements in group 13 could exist in a liquid state during summer?
questions.explanation: Ga has a very low melting point (MP = 29.76^oC), and because of that, it can exist in a liquid state during summer.

questions.question: The relative stability of +1 oxidation state progressively increases for heavier elements. Choose one of the following having most stable +1 oxidation state.
questions.question_raw: The relative stability of +1 oxidation state progressively increases for heavier elements. Choose one of the following having most stable +1 oxidation state.
questions.explanation: Due to the inert pair effect, Tl^+1 is the most stable oxidation state.

questions.question: Aluminium when exposed to air protects itself from further attack by:
questions.question_raw: Aluminium when exposed to air protects itself from further attack by:

options.label: A
options.text: reacting with air and liberating dihydrogen.

options.label: B
options.text: forming a very thin oxide layer on its surface.

options.label: D
options.text: reacting with hydrogen in the air.

correct_option_texts: forming a very thin oxide layer on its surface.
questions.explanation: Al forms a thick layer of oxide (Al_2O_3) on its surface which protects it from further attack.

questions.question: White fumes appear around the bottle of anhydrous aluminium chloride. Why?
questions.question_raw: White fumes appear around the bottle of anhydrous aluminium chloride. Why?

options.label: A
options.text: Formation of HCl on reaction with atmospheric moisture and then Aluminium dissolves in dilute HCl and liberates dihydrogen.

options.label: B
options.text: Formation of HCl on reaction with atmospheric moisture and then Aluminium reacts with dilute HCl and forms aluminium chloride.

options.label: C
options.text: Liberation of HCl on reaction with atmospheric moisture.

options.label: D
options.text: Formation of dihydrogen on reaction with atmospheric moisture.

correct_option_texts: Liberation of HCl on reaction with atmospheric moisture.
questions.explanation:
White fumes appear because of the formation of HCl on reaction with atmospheric moisture. White fumes appear due to the formation of HCl gas. Anhydrous aluminium chloride is partly hydrolyzed by the moisture in the atmosphere and produces HCl gas. This HCl further combines with the moisture of the air and appears white fumes.
AlCl_3 +3H_2O $\longrightarrow$ Al(OH)_3 +3HCl

questions.question: Diborane is produced on an industrial scale by the reaction of BF_3 with:
questions.question_raw: Diborane is produced on an industrial scale by the reaction of BF3 with:
questions.explanation:
Reaction of BF_3 with NaH produces B_2H_6 At 450K following reaction happens
2BF_3 + 6NaH $\longrightarrow$ B_2H_6 + 6NaF

tests.testPaperName: The p Block Elements CBSE Online Test 02
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: The angular shape of ozone molecules (O_3) consists of
questions.question_raw: The angular shape of ozone molecules (O3) consists of
questions.explanation: The ozone structure will have a single bond, a double bond, and two resonance structure, therefore, O_3 consists of 2 sigma bond and 1 pie bond.

questions.question: Boric acid is an acid because of its molecule:
questions.question_raw: Boric acid is an acid because of its molecule:

options.label: A
options.text: accepts OH^- from water releasing proton.

options.label: D
options.text: combines with proton from water molecule.

correct_option_texts: accepts OH^- from water releasing proton.
questions.explanation: Because of the small size of the boron atom and the presence of only six electrons in its valence shell, B(OH)_3 accepts a pair of electrons from OH^- ion of H_2O, releasing a proton.

questions.question: Silicon is the second most abundant element on the earth’s crust and is a component of:
questions.question_raw: Silicon is the second most abundant element on the earth’s crust and is a component of:
questions.explanation: Si is the most imp component of ceramics, which is comprised of metal, non-metal, or metalloids held by an ionic or covalent bond.

questions.question: Carbon is most widely distributed in nature. In the combined state, it is available as:
questions.question_raw: Carbon is most widely distributed in nature. In the combined state, it is available as:
questions.explanation: In combined state C can occur as hydrocarbons (which is a compound of carbon and hydrogen).

questions.question: Stable isotopes of carbon are:
questions.question_raw: Stable isotopes of carbon are:
questions.explanation: C^12 and C^13 are the most abundant isotopes of Carbon which are very stable. Other isotopes like C^14 is radioactive (disintegrate to give other products).

questions.question: The elements of group 14 are slightly more electronegative than group 13 elements because of:
questions.question_raw: The elements of group 14 are slightly more electronegative than group 13 elements because of:
questions.explanation: As we move along the period electronegativity increases due to an increase in nuclear charge and a decrease in size.

questions.question: In general, non-metals as compared to metals have:
questions.question_raw: In general, non-metals as compared to metals have:

options.label: A
options.text: Lower covalent character of the compounds formed between them

questions.explanation:
Non-metals have a high value of ionization energy while metals have a comparatively low value of ionization energy.
As we move across a period (Metal are there in the left-hand side and nonmetals are there in the right-hand side), the ionization energy of an element increases. This is because of the effective nuclear charge increases.

questions.question: The compounds formed by highly reactive non-metals with highly reactive metals are generally:
questions.question_raw: The compounds formed by highly reactive non-metals with highly reactive metals are generally:

options.label: A
options.text: Slightly different in their ionisation enthalpies

questions.explanation: Compounds formed by highly reactive non-metals and metals is an ionic bond. Here metal will loose electron to form cation and nonmetal will gain an electron to form an anion.

questions.question: The non-metal oxides are:
questions.question_raw: The non-metal oxides are:
questions.explanation: Nonmetallic oxides are either neutral or acidic. Example CO, NO, H_2O is neutral while CO_2 is acidic.

questions.question: The element having the noble gas plus 14 f- electrons plus 10 d-electron cores______.
questions.question_raw: The element having the noble gas plus 14 f- electrons plus 10 d-electron cores______.
questions.explanation:
Thallium has noble gas + 14 f +10 d electrons.
[Xe] 4f^_14 5d^_10 6s^_2 6p^_1 . Thus the electronic structure of these element is more complex.

questions.question: The element having the noble gas plus 10 d-electron cores________.
questions.question_raw: The element having the noble gas plus 10 d-electron cores________.
questions.explanation: Both gallium and Indium has noble gas +10 d electrons [Ar] 3d^10 4s^2 4p^1 and [ Kr] 4d^105s^25p^1 respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Lewis acid?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is a Lewis acid?
questions.explanation: AlCl_3 is lewis acid. Group 13 halides are lewis acid because of having incompletely filled orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following oxides is acidic in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following oxides is acidic in nature?
questions.explanation: As we go down the group metallic character increases. So most acidic oxide is the oxide of most non metallic element, i.e. B_2O_3

questions.question: Boron from covalent Compound due to
questions.question_raw: Boron from covalent Compound due to

options.label: B
options.text: higher ionisation enthalpy and Small size

correct_option_texts: higher ionisation enthalpy and Small size
questions.explanation: higher ionisation enthalpy and Small size

questions.question: The geometry of a complex species can be understood from the knowledge of the type of hybridization of orbitals of the central atom. The hybridization of orbitals of the central atom in $\left[ {{\text{Be}}{{\left( {{\text{OH}}} \right)}_{\text{4}}}} \right]$ and the geometry of the complex is respectively.
questions.question_raw: The geometry of a complex species can be understood from the knowledge of the type of hybridization of orbitals of the central atom. The hybridization of orbitals of the central atom in {tex}\left[ {{\text{Be}}{{\left( {{\text{OH}}} \right)}_{\text{4}}}} \right]{/tex} and the geometry of the complex is respectively.
questions.explanation: In [Be(OH)_4] Be is tetrahedrally surrounded by 4 OH^- ion.

tests.testPaperName: The p Block Elements CBSE Online Test 03
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: Catenation i.e., linking of similar atoms depends on size and electronic configuration of atoms. The tendency of catenation in Group 14 elements follows the order:
questions.question_raw: Catenation i.e., linking of similar atoms depends on size and electronic configuration of atoms. The tendency of catenation in Group 14 elements follows the order:
questions.explanation: Catenation property decreases down the group as size of the atom increases.

questions.question: Silicon has a strong tendency to form polymers like silicones. The chain length of silicone polymer can be controlled by adding:
questions.question_raw: Silicon has a strong tendency to form polymers like silicones. The chain length of silicone polymer can be controlled by adding:
questions.explanation: Me_3SiCl has only one Cl and (can react once) to forms only dimer thus it is used as a chain controlling agent (terminate the polymerisation process).

questions.question: In the structure of diborane:
questions.question_raw: In the structure of diborane:

options.label: A
options.text: All hydrogen atoms lie in one plane and boron atoms lie in a plane perpendicular to this plane.

options.label: B
options.text: 2 boron atoms and 4 terminal hydrogen atoms lie in the same plane and 2 bridging hydrogen atoms lie in the perpendicular plane.

options.label: C
options.text: All the atoms are in the same plane.

options.label: D
options.text: 4 bridging hydrogen atoms and boron atoms lie in one plane and two terminal hydrogen atoms lie in a plane perpendicular to this plane.

correct_option_texts: 2 boron atoms and 4 terminal hydrogen atoms lie in the same plane and 2 bridging hydrogen atoms lie in the perpendicular plane.
questions.explanation: B_2H_6 has 4 terminal H attached to B atoms and all these lies in the same plane. Also in diborane, we have 2 bridging hydrogens (3 centre 2 electrons) which lie perpendicular to the plane.

questions.question: Quartz is extensively used as a piezoelectric material, it contains ___________.
questions.question_raw: Quartz is extensively used as a piezoelectric material, it contains ___________.
questions.explanation: Quartz contains Si. [Piezoelectric materials produces electricity when pressure is applied]

questions.question: A compound X, of boron, reacts with NH_3 on heating to give another compound. Y which is called inorganic benzene. The compound X can be prepared by treating BF_3 with Lithium aluminium hydride. The compounds X and Y are represented by the formulas.
questions.question_raw: A compound X, of boron, reacts with NH3 on heating to give another compound. Y which is called inorganic benzene. The compound X can be prepared by treating BF3 with Lithium aluminium hydride. The compounds X and Y are represented by the formulas.
questions.explanation:
BF_3 reacts with LiAlH_4 to form B_2H_6 which reacts with NH_3 in 1:2 ratio forming B_3N_3H_6 (Borazine, inorganic benzene). Reaction is as below:
3B_2H_6 + 6NH_3 $\rightarrow$ 2B_3N_3H_6 + 12H_2
Preparation of B_2H_6
4BF_3 + 3LiAlH_4 $\rightarrow$ 2B_2H_6 + 3LiF + 3AlF_3.

questions.question: The linear shape of CO_2 is due to _________.
questions.question_raw: The linear shape of CO2 is due to _________.

options.label: D
options.text: p$\pi$– p$\pi $ bonding between carbon and oxygen

correct_option_texts: p$\pi$– p$\pi $ bonding between carbon and oxygen
questions.explanation: In CO_2 (​​​​O=C=O) hybridization of C is sp so due to p$\pi$ –p$\pi$ bonding it is linear. Further carbon is sp hybridized, and have a linear structure.

questions.question: The reason for the small radius of Ga compared to Al is:
questions.question_raw: The reason for the small radius of Ga compared to Al is:

options.label: B
options.text: Poor screening effect of d and f orbitals

correct_option_texts: Poor screening effect of d and f orbitals
questions.explanation:
The presence of an additional 10d- electrons offers a poor screening effect for the outer electrons from the increased nuclear charge in gallium.
[Electronic configuration of Ga is [Ar] 3d^10 4s^2 4p^1, that of aluminum is [Ne]3s^23p^1].

questions.question: Cement, the important building material is a mixture of oxides of several elements. Besides iron and sulphur, oxides of elements of which of the group (s) are present in the mixture?
questions.question_raw: Cement, the important building material is a mixture of oxides of several elements. Besides iron and sulphur, oxides of elements of which of the group (s) are present in the mixture?
questions.explanation:
Main Composition of Portland cement are: lime (CaO) (group 2), silica (SiO _2)(group 14) and alumina (Al _2O _3).(group 13) 
Cement is a heavy powder of calcium aluminates and silicates

questions.question: Dry ice is:
questions.question_raw: Dry ice is:
questions.explanation: Dry ice is solid CO_2. Solid CO_2 sublimates. Thus it does not wet anything and called dry ice.

questions.question: In trivalent state, for example, the trichlorides, being covalent are hydrolysed in water form:
questions.question_raw: In trivalent state, for example, the trichlorides, being covalent are hydrolysed in water form:
questions.explanation: Trihalides hydrolysis to form a tetrahedral structure with four covalent bonds.

questions.question: Diamond is covalent, yet it has a high melting point because of:
questions.question_raw: Diamond is covalent, yet it has a high melting point because of:

options.label: A
options.text: the presence of rigid three dimensional extended networks thought the lattice.

options.label: B
options.text: sp^2 hybridisation with strong C-C bonds difficult to break.

options.label: C
options.text: planar structure with strong C-C bonds difficult to break.

options.label: D
options.text: 2-dimensional network with strong C-C bonds difficult to break.

correct_option_texts: the presence of rigid three dimensional extended networks thought the lattice.
questions.explanation: Carbon atom undergoes sp^3 hybridization and linked to four other carbon atoms by using hybridized orbitals in tetrahedral fashion-led a rigid three-dimensional extended network thought the lattice.

questions.question: The valence shell electronic configuration of p-block elements is ns^2np^1 - 6. But this is not true in the case of ______.
questions.question_raw: The valence shell electronic configuration of p-block elements is ns2np1 - 6. But this is not true in the case of ______.
questions.explanation: As only one I^st period element of p-block, it has unique 1s^2 configuration besides another element of that block (ns^2np^1 - 6). It is a noble gas.

questions.question: Cohen Orthoboric acid (H_3BO_3) is strongly heated, the residue is
questions.question_raw: Cohen Orthoboric acid (H3BO3) is strongly heated, the residue is
questions.explanation: Meta boric acid

questions.question: Me_3SiCl is used during polymerization of organosilicones because:
questions.question_raw: Me3SiCl is used during polymerization of organosilicones because:

options.label: A
options.text: Me_3 SiCl does not block the end terminal of the silicone polymer.

options.label: B
options.text: Me_3 SiCl acts as a catalyst during polymerisation.

options.label: C
options.text: Me_3 SiCl improves the quality and yield of the polymer.

options.label: D
options.text: to control the chain length of the polymer.

correct_option_texts: to control the chain length of the polymer.
questions.explanation: The chain length of the polymers can be controlled by adding Me_3SiCl which blocks the ends of the chain to produce silicone.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are correct?
 Fullerenes are cage-like molecules.
 Fullerenes have dangling bonds.
 Graphite is thermodynamically not stable allotrope of carbon.
 Graphite is slippery and hard and therefore used as a dry lubricant in Machines.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statements are correct?

  1. Fullerenes are cage-like molecules.
  2. Fullerenes have dangling bonds.
  3. Graphite is thermodynamically not stable allotrope of carbon.
  4. Graphite is slippery and hard and therefore used as a dry lubricant in Machines.
questions.explanation: Fullerene has a cage-like spheroidal molecular (C_60) structure and has no dangling bonds.

tests.testPaperName: The p Block Elements CBSE Online Test 04
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: Which one of the following orders correctly represents the increasing acid strengths of the given acids?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the  following orders correctly represents the increasing acid strengths of the given acids?
questions.explanation: Acidity increases as the number of a terminal oxygen atom attached to the halogen (Cl) atom because of the inductive-effect.

questions.question: Regular use of which of the following fertilizers increases the acidity of the soil?
questions.question_raw: Regular use of which of the following fertilizers increases the acidity of the soil?
questions.explanation:
Ammonium sulphate addition increases acidity in the soil.
In the presence of water and oxygen in the soil, ammonium and sulfate are decomposed by naturally occurring microbes. During this process, acids are released into the soil, along with nitrogen and sulfur compounds.

questions.question: The percentage of p-character in the orbitals forming P - P bonds in P_4 is______.
questions.question_raw: The percentage of p-character in the orbitals forming P - P bonds in P4 is______.
questions.explanation: The species P_4 has a tetrahedron structure. Each P involves sp^3 hybrid orbital. Hence p-character will be 75.

questions.question: When Cl_2 gas reacts with hot and concentrated sodium hydroxide solution, the oxidation number of chlorine changes from:
questions.question_raw: When Cl2 gas reacts with hot and concentrated sodium hydroxide solution, the oxidation number of chlorine changes from:
questions.explanation:
Reaction of Cl_2 and hot and concentrated sodium hydroxide solution is as below:
6 NaOH + 3Cl_2 → 5NaCl + NaClO_3 + 3H_2O
Here Cl_2 disproportionate to -1 and +5

questions.question: In which of the following compounds, nitrogen exhibits highest oxidation state?
questions.question_raw: In which of the following compounds, nitrogen exhibits highest oxidation state?
questions.explanation: N_3H is Hydrazoic acid and can be seen as N_3^- H+.

questions.question:
Three reactions involving ${{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}$$O_4^ - $ are given below
 ${{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{\text{P}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{4}}} + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + } + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}o_4^ - $
 ${{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}o_4^ - + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{ HP}}O_2^ + + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + }$
 ${{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}O_4^ - + O{H^ - }?H3PO4 + O_2^ - $
In which of the above does ${{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}$P$O_4^ - $ act as an acid?
questions.question_raw:
Three reactions involving {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}{/tex}{tex}O_4^ - {/tex} are given below

  1. {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{\text{P}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{4}}} + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + } + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}o_4^ - {/tex}
  2. {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}o_4^ - + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{ HP}}O_2^ + + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + }{/tex}
  3. {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{P}}O_4^ - + O{H^ - }?H3PO4 + O_2^ - {/tex}

In which of the above does {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{/tex}P{tex}O_4^ - {/tex} act as an acid?
questions.explanation: In B only $H_2P O_4^ - $ is losing its proton therefore act as acid.

questions.question: The correct order of increasing bond angles in the following species is:
questions.question_raw: The correct order of increasing bond angles in the following species is:
questions.explanation:
The correct order of increasing bond angles in the following species is ${\text{Cl}}O_2^ - < {\text{ C}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} < {\text{ Cl}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}$
lp-lp repulsion and lp-bp repulsion is responsible behind it.

questions.question: The tendency of BF_3, BCl_3, and BBr_3 to behave as Lewis acid decreases in the sequence:
questions.question_raw: The tendency of BF3, BCl3, and BBr3 to behave as Lewis acid decreases in the sequence:
questions.explanation: The strength of Lewis acid is in the order of BBr_3 > BCl_3 > BF_3. as the size of halogen, acid increases the acidic strength of boron halide increases thous BF_3 is the weakest acid This is because of the tendency of the halogen atom to form back bonding by its lone pairs of electrons to the vacant p-orbitals of boron. In BF_3 this back bonding is formed by the overlap of 2p-2p orbital (same energy). Thus the tendency to accept lone pair (Lewis acid property) decreases. But in the case of BBr_3 and BI_3 the back bond is formed by p orbitals of different energy, thus less effective.

questions.question: P_4O_10 is the anhydride of:
questions.question_raw: P4O10 is the anhydride of:
questions.explanation:
P_4O_10 is the anhydride of H_3PO_4
P_4O_10 + 6 H_2O $\longrightarrow$ 4 H_3PO_4

questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding ozone is not correct?
 The oxygen-oxygen bond length in ozone is identical with that of molecular oxygen
 The ozone is response hybrid of two structures
 The ozone molecule is angular in shape
 Ozone is used as a germicide and disinfectant for the purification of air.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statements regarding ozone is not correct?

  1. The oxygen-oxygen bond length in ozone is identical with that of molecular oxygen
  2. The ozone is response hybrid of two structures
  3. The ozone molecule is angular in shape
  4. Ozone is used as a germicide and disinfectant for the purification of air.
questions.explanation:
Bond order of O-O bond in ozone is less than that in oxygen.
Ozone molecule can be represented as a resonance hybrid of two canonical structures, each structure has one single bond on one side and a double bond on the another side.

questions.question: Which of the following contains P- O - P bond?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following contains P- O - P bond?
questions.explanation: Pyrophoshoric acid (also known as diphosphoric acid) contains P-O-P bond. The molecular formula of pyrophosphoric acid is H_4P_2O_7.

questions.question: Which noble gas is most abundant in the atmosphere?
questions.question_raw: Which noble gas is most abundant in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: There's so much Ar in the atmosphere because most of it was produced by a long-lived radioactive isotope of potassium in the earth's crust and released over geologic time.

questions.question: The reaction of P_4 with X leads selectively to P_4O_6. The X is:
questions.question_raw: The reaction of P4 with X leads selectively to P4O6. The X is:

options.label: B
options.text: A mixture of O_2 and N_2

options.label: D
options.text: O_2 in the presence of aqueous NaOH

correct_option_texts: A mixture of O_2 and N_2
questions.explanation: P_4 + 3O_2 $\longrightarrow$P_4O_6( in presence of N_2 ). N_2 is used to retard further oxidation).

questions.question: Which of the following reactions of xenon compounds is not feasible?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following reactions of xenon compounds is not feasible?
questions.explanation: 1st reaction is not feasible. Rather the opposite reaction happens XeF_6 + 3 H_2O $\longrightarrow$ XeO_3 + 6 HF.

questions.question: In which of the following arrangements, the sequence is not strictly according to the property written against it?
questions.question_raw: In which of the following arrangements, the sequence is not strictly according to the property written against it?

options.label: A
options.text: B < C < O < N: Increasing first ionization enthalpy

options.label: B
options.text: HF < HCl < HBr < HI :Increasing acid strength

options.label: C
options.text: NH_3 < PH_3< AsH_3 < SbH_3: Increasing basic strength

options.label: D
options.text: CO_2 < SiO_2 < SnO_2 < PbO_2: Increasing oxidizing power

correct_option_texts: NH_3 < PH_3< AsH_3 < SbH_3: Increasing basic strength
questions.explanation: As we move down the group basicity decreases. As we go down the group size of atom increase, the availability of lone pairs decreases. Because it occupies a larger volume. Thus the basic character of hydrides decreases as follows: NH_3>PH_3>AsH_3>SbH_3.

tests.testPaperName: The p Block Elements CBSE Online Test 05
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > Some p-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: Among the following which is the strongest oxidizing agent?
questions.question_raw: Among the following which is the strongest oxidizing agent?
questions.explanation: F_2 is the most electronegative element hence it readily get reduced to F^- ion so it is a very strong oxidizing agent.

questions.question: The brown ring test for nitrates depends on:
questions.question_raw: The brown ring test for nitrates depends on:

options.label: A
options.text: reduction of nitrate to nitric oxide.

options.label: B
options.text: oxidation of nitric oxide to nitrogen dioxide.

options.label: D
options.text: the reduction of ferrous sulphate to iron.

correct_option_texts: reduction of nitrate to nitric oxide.
questions.explanation:
In the ring test of nitrates, nitrate ion is reduced to nitric oxide. The reaction is as below:
$2HN{O_3} + 3{H_2}S{O_4} + 6FeS{O_4} \to 3F{e_2}{(S{O_4})_3} + 2NO + 4{H_2}O$
$[Fe{({H_2}O)_6}]S{O_4} + NO \to [Fe{({H_2}O)_5}(NO)]S{O_4} + {H_2}O$

questions.question: One mole of magnesium nitride on the reaction with an excess of water gives:
questions.question_raw: One mole of magnesium nitride on the reaction with an excess of water gives:

questions.question: Which of the following statements is not valid for oxoacids of phosphorus?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements is not valid for oxoacids of phosphorus?

options.label: A
options.text: Hypophosphorous acid is a diprotic acid.

options.label: B
options.text: All oxoacids contain tetrahedral four coordinated phosphorus.

options.label: C
options.text: All oxoacids contain at least one P = O unit and one P – OH group.

options.label: D
options.text: Orthophosphoric acid is used in the manufacture of triple superphosphate.

correct_option_texts: Hypophosphorous acid is a diprotic acid.
questions.explanation: Hypophosphorous acid is a phosphorus oxoacid and a powerful reducing agent with molecular formula H_3PO_2. Inorganic chemists refer to the free acid by this name (also as "HPA"), or the acceptable name of phosphinic acid. It is a colorless, Monoprotic low-melting compound, which is soluble in water, dioxane, and alcohol.

questions.question: Sulphur trioxide can be obtained by which of the following reaction:
questions.question_raw: Sulphur trioxide can be obtained by which of the following reaction:
questions.explanation: Thermal decomposition of FeSO_4 produces the following products $2FeSO_4 \to Fe_2O_3 + SO_2 + SO_3$.

questions.question: Argon is used:
questions.question_raw: Argon is used:

options.label: B
options.text: In radio therapy for treatment of cancer

questions.explanation: Ar is used in high temperature welding to provide an inert atmosphere.

questions.question: The function of Fe(OH)_3 in the contact process is:
questions.question_raw: The function of Fe(OH)3 in the contact process is:
questions.explanation: Fe(OH)_3 is used to remove arsenic impurities.

questions.question: Of the following compounds the most acidic is:
questions.question_raw: Of the following compounds the most acidic is:
questions.explanation: More is the oxidation number greater is the acidity of the oxide. Here P is in +5 oxidation state.

questions.question: Atoms in a P_4 molecule of white phosphorus are arranged regularly in the following way:
questions.question_raw: Atoms in a P4 molecule of white phosphorus are arranged regularly in the following way:

options.label: A
options.text: At the corners of a tetrahedron

options.label: B
options.text: At the corners of the cube

options.label: C
options.text: At the corners of the octahedron

options.label: D
options.text: At the centre and corners of a tetrahedron

correct_option_texts: At the corners of a tetrahedron
questions.explanation: in P_4 four P atoms are arranged at the corners of tetrahedron (tetrahedral structure).

questions.question: Aqueous solution of Na_2S_2O_3 on reaction with Cl_2 gives______.
questions.question_raw: Aqueous solution of Na2S2O3 on reaction with Cl2 gives______.
questions.explanation:
Aqueous Na_2S_2O_3 on reaction with Cl_2 produces NaHSO_4.
Reaction is given below:
$Na_2S_2O_3 + 4Cl_2 + 5H_2O \to 2NaHSO_4 + 8HCl$

questions.question: The angular shape of ozone molecules $\left( {{{\text{O}}_{\text{3}}}} \right)$ consists of
questions.question_raw: The angular shape of ozone molecules {tex}\left( {{{\text{O}}_{\text{3}}}} \right){/tex} consists of
questions.explanation:
since both bonds are identical, identical in length and strength. This is due to sp^2 hybridization and delocalized pi bonding. Each oxygen has an unhybridized p-orbital, and all three overlap side to side, producing a sigma bond and a pi bond between the central oxygen atom and each of the terminal oxygen atoms. In this case, there are two sigma bonds and two pi bonds. 
Therefore, it will contain two sigma bonds and one pi bond.

questions.question: Which one of the following arrangement does not give the correct picture of the trends indicated against it?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following arrangement does not give the correct picture of the trends indicated against it?

options.label: A
options.text: Cl_2 > Br_2 > F_2 > I_2 Bond dissociation energy

options.label: C
options.text: F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2: Electron gain enthalpy

options.label: D
options.text: F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2: Oxidizing power

correct_option_texts: Cl_2 > Br_2 > F_2 > I_2 Bond dissociation energy
questions.explanation: Correct order of Electron gain enthalpy of halogens is Cl_2 > F_2 > Br_2 > I_2. This is because of the very small size of fluorine.

questions.question:
Four species are listed below:
 $HCO_3^ - $
 ${{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + }$
 $HSO_4^ - $
 ${\text{HS}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{3}}}{\text{F}}$
Which one of the following is the correct sequence of their acidic strength?
questions.question_raw:
Four species are listed below:

  1. {tex}HCO_3^ - {/tex}
  2. {tex}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{{\text{O}}^ + }{/tex}
  3. {tex}HSO_4^ - {/tex}
  4. {tex}{\text{HS}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{3}}}{\text{F}}{/tex}

Which one of the following is the correct sequence of their acidic strength?
questions.explanation:
i < iii < ii < iv is the order of acidic strength.
as IV is stabilised by -I effect of fluorine.

questions.question: Helium is used in balloons in place of hydrogen because it is:
questions.question_raw: Helium is used in balloons in place of hydrogen because it is:
questions.explanation: He is used in balloons in place of hydrogenas because He is incombustible, but hydrogen is highly combustible gas.

questions.question: Which is most easily liquefiable rare gas?
questions.question_raw: Which is most easily liquefiable rare gas?
questions.explanation: Xe is most easily liquefiable rare gas. Xenon has a high critical temperature. Thus it is easily liquefiable.
